title: The Pinned Knight
setup: A black knight on **e7** stands frozen between the white rook on e2 and the black king on e8.
hint: A pinned knight is the saddest knight — it cannot even hop away.
explanation:
## The Idea

**Absolute pin** on a knight. Normally knights laugh at pins because they jump, but a piece pinned to its own king cannot legally move at all — even to a non-capturing square — because the move would expose the king.

White plays {{Rxe7+}}, winning the knight cleanly.

- {{Kxe7}} is the only legal reply.
- The trade is rook (5) for knight (3). At first glance that looks like a loss, but in this barren endgame White wins by simplifying into king-and-rook vs lone king.
- In a richer position, this same pattern just **wins a knight for nothing** when more material is on the board.

## Pattern to Memorize

> A pinned knight is helpless: it cannot move, cannot defend, cannot escape.

Whenever you see an enemy knight on the same line as its king, look for ways to **add an attacker** to that square.
